channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-365] Update copyright messages for files modified by Whamcloud
item.description:
The copyright headers for files modified by Whamcloud engineers should be updated with a new copyright message in addition to the existing Oracle copyright message (which should not be removed).

Copyright (C) 2011, Whamcloud, Inc.
item.summary: Update copyright messages for files modified by Whamcloud

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
This bug needs to be updated to include both the 2011 changes, and now also 2012 changes, like:

  • Copyright (c) 2011, 2012 Whamcloud, Inc.

I was going to close this bug, but it seems there are still quite a number of files that are modified since Lustre 2.1 that do not have Whamcloud copyright messages in them:

git diff v2_1_0_0..HEAD | diffstat | sed -e 's# b/# #' |
     awk '{ print $1 }' | grep "\.[ch]$" | sort --uniq | while read F; do
        grep -q "2011.*Wham" "$F" || echo "$F"
     done

returns 94 files that did not get a copyright, while 98 DID get a copyright. The list isn't 100% correct, since it may include files that were modified by someone outside Whamcloud but at least provides the list of files that should be checked.

Integrated in lustre-master » i686,server,el5,ofa #271
LU-365 Update lustre/Changelog

Oleg Drokin : 802aeb09afc39206ec961dc995c0e19856548eeb
Files :

  • lustre/ChangeLog

Integrated in lustre-master » i686,client,el5,inkernel #271
LU-365 Update lustre/Changelog

Oleg Drokin : 802aeb09afc39206ec961dc995c0e19856548eeb
Files :

  • lustre/ChangeLog

It wouldn't be a terrible idea to add the copyright message into the files that we modified with a WC-developed patch.
It looks like this has happened for 2.1 but does 1.8.6-wc need these same changes?

Integrated in lustre-master » x86_64,client,el5,inkernel #142
LU-365 cleanup old irrelevant files

Oleg Drokin : 6ca1679c3499513789132441fb7f8b7a89efe5e2
Files :

  • build/diffatt.sh
  • build/misc/templates/file_header.c
  • build/branch.sh
  • build/pkginfo
  • build/prototype

Yes, I was looking at that. I was going to delete it as part of the change #860 patch, but then decided it is better to stick around. However, I'm not sure it makes sense to commit a version to the Lustre repo that adds the Whamcloud copyright, but the script could easily be made generic enough so that it adds a copyright based on a supplied argument.

The only other concern is that we would need to check that all of the changed files were actually changed by WC and not by someone else, so I'm not sure it can be done in an automated fashion, unless it also checks each commit for the principal "Signed-off-by" email being someone at whamcloud.com, but even that is not necessarily a 100% guarantee, since we could have accepted plain patches from someone else and only gotten the first Signed-off-by line when it was added to Gerrit (though that shouldn't happen in theory).
Perhaps we can repurpose the build/updatecw.sh script to add our copyright lines on files we've changed.
While looking for an example copyright message, I also found a bunch of old cruft in the build/ directory. I submitted http://review.whamcloud.com/860 to clean up the old files.
